sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI I.T.R.A. No. 452 of 2024 Date Order with Signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For order on office objection No.25&27 2. For order on CMA No.3707/2024 (exemption) 3. For hearing of Main Case 29.01.2026 Mr. Faheem Raza, advocate for applicant On the last date, the following order was passed:- “Learned counsel seeks time to reformulate the questions of law. At his request, to come up after four weeks”. Despite passage of four weeks, learned counsel has not filed any new questions of law and merely seeks time. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported in PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of the Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Ashraf
